title: elymus repens subsp. repens
intro: Elymus repens subsp. repens, also known as creeping couch grass or dog's tail grass, is a perennial grass native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its creeping rhizomes and long, narrow leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which can be attributed to its high content of saponins and essential oils. Elymus repens subsp. repens has been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting immune function. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to treat a range of health issues, including respiratory problems and skin conditions.
